reviews.review_text:
Lovely little villa we stayed in. Needed some extra warmth (blankets and doona would go far)
Oven didn’t work and needed a strainer(for pasta). Could also use a lamp for the ambiance but lovely place all round.
Oh , loved staying at this place . So clean in which I really appreciated. Very pleasant to stay at and highly recommended it . Breaky at the park was yum . Well worth it .

The retreat - villas
Simply the BEST (ever)
VERY NEW, modern units.
Fabulous beds, well appointed, many inclusions.
Beautiful, spacious cabins with full amenities.  Loved the gas log heater. Close to town.

This would have to be the noisiest park we have stayed at. Before booking we looked at the map and mistakenly thought Churchill Ave was not a main road as it only appeared to go towards the small settlement if Wandiligong.  How wrong were we!  This roads leads to an industrial area and also services the forestry industry.  Trucks start up at 5:30am roaring past.

Yesterday we had the council chainsawing and chipping at tree from 7:30am.

Tonight we have road workers in a back cabin blasting us with their music.

Pay a little more to find a park off any sort of main road.  I wish someone had written this review for us before we booked.

None of this if the fault of park management, it is simply the location.  Having said this, sites are very small and we have never been asked by cleaning staff as they whizz past, if we need anything. 1 and 1/2 rolls of toilet paper did not last 9 days and we needed to buy more.

Smelly, just like a an Uber taxi which is not cleaned inside.
Very old torn dirty couch which has taken and kept from the rubbish and a not properly cleaned toilet.The  curtain at the entrance is dirty and smelly and a smelly cushion was kept in the bed too.
When you make a booking nice photos are there in the page but they are misleading.
Very dissappointed and annoyed

The luxury villas are a great place for a weekend getaway.

Look at booking early as they book out pretty well.

Great little town to go around for an away from city feel.

I remember staying at this park as a kid and now I get to bring my child here it was very nostalgic. We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin one of the older style. Cannot complain had everything we needed. We unfortunately had a cabin of what sounded like baby elephants stomping around all night and then again early morning next to us and despite repeatedly asking them to quiet down, were met with resistance from the guests. This is not the parks fault at all, just zero etiquette for your fellow travellers when sharing a holiday park.
The team at the front desk were super lovely upon check in and out and helpful for any questions we had during our stay. Cabins had most things you would need to use in the kitchen. Favourite part was the super long undercover porch which was perfect for entertaining!
And despite the weather being 13 degrees and windy the kids enjoyed the pool and the following hypothermia haha would happily return!

Great caravan park. Camp kitchen was a fantastic area with a lounge and TV ABC on for the kids overlooking a electric fireplace and Pool table and ping pong table.

Coffee van onsite with good coffee and great food... was abit expensive but not too bad.

Toilet and showers very clean when we first got there however after 3 nights I noticed the standards started to drop off.

Rained for the last 2 days the park arranged a lady to come in and do fun activity for the kids to keep them entertained. Bubbles colouring in etc etc.

Roads throughout the park could do with abit of asphalt maintaince as some areas are causing safety issues.

As many reviews have mentioned the area is tight when busy to reverse a van into. Had to ask other guests to move there car for us to get into our camp.

Still a great park have left a honest review based on our experience. We will be back.

Had an amazing stay, facilities were all well kept and all staff extremely friendly. In particular our boys both love Justine going above and beyond for them.

Only minor complaint was some cabins having 2-3 cars in parks making it difficult to get caravans into/out of small sites but that's really on the people staying in those cabins and was still manageable.

Absolutely adore this park. We had the best time while we were there but my daughter left her little reindeer toy behind. After searching for a couple of months, we reached out just to see and he was their little office buddy! They were so amazing, sent it back with the most gorgeous card and photo of his adventures 🥰 Such amazing and lovely staff, we absolutely adore them!! X
Bright is very special. The riverside park is stunning for all ages! The markets are super
Enjoyed our stay in their 3 bedroom villa which is modern and well equipped. Loved the shower! Hot and powerful and perfect after a long day on the trails.

Modern style cabin, love the rooms, except the kids room/bunk beds, there is no aircorn or fan. 4 kids in there for 3 hot nights was crazy. super clean cabins. Overall its great, will be back for winter.

We are pleasantly surprised on just how amazing the place was.  The amenities, accessibility and location honestly was outstanding. The cabin was clean and met our needs perfectly.  We barely left the cabin in the evening. Everything we wanted was right there.
To be honest my sister stayed in a cabin all of ants within in cabin

We stayed in a 2BR villa cabin, my toddler loved the pool and how spacious the rooms are. My hubby and I enjoyed our stay and the convenience of a coffee van opposite the reception. Highly recommend this gem

Really enjoyed our stay over the January holiday period. Was very happy with the amenities and cabin we stayed in.......until I went to rebook.
The price jumped from $339 a night to $429 ($409) with return discount, and then told we now have to book a minimum of 5 nights. So our stay jumped from $1356 to $2045! $2165 without discount. That's a 26% yearly increase.
I understand inflation, insurance increases, wages, and demand. But that sort of increase is price gouging, especially for a caravan park. Management was unapologetic.

We thoroughly enjoyed our first ever visit to Bright and stay at Tasman - South Bright! Communication from the team was fantastic from our first enquiry and throughout our stay! We remained at the park during the recent hot days and bushfires and the team ensured we were well informed and kept up to date with all info and emergency warnings. Facilities are well maintained and location is fantastic with just a short drive or walk into town. We would visit again!

Cabins are pretty ratty, but I guess they are marked as budget and pet friendly, for the cost though during peak times is still A LOT for what it is.
Couple of gripes,cutlery and crockery were still dirty,antswere meaning the cabin, the blinds are terrible, there needs to be more shade options at the pool, and the kids play area needs a shade sail desperately! It's not usable. Then there's the dust and gravel not a blade of grass to be had.
Still, they allow dogs and are close to town so will likely be back, but each time I think it's a massively overpriced.

Ohh and our booking flip flopped multiple times and there were several errors that were just annoying.

Beautifully presented holiday park. Park is very tight when trying to get your van into site when it’s busy. We had to interrupt other guests staying to get them to move their cars to accommodate space needed for our 19.6 van. We visited during a heatwave and the pool was over crowded causing safety issues with kids sliding into each other, jumping on each other ect.

We rented a basic family cabin. There were higher grade newer luxury villas but we were looking to save money. |Everything worked in the cabin but taps dripped, window fly screen was broken, paint work was in need of some care and attention. |It may be that the cabin was due to be replaced by the newer luxury models but if not it needs work. |Very convenient location with a pool and bike cage on site. Also has a coffee caravan but we did not try. | Bright has its own swimming hole anyway so we also did not use the pool but there were plenty of kids loving it.
Very nice and clean park. Located in the heart of Bright.

The Delaney Retreat villas attached to Tasman Holiday Park Sth Bright felt very exclusive and luxe.  There was spacious parking enough for 2 cars or 1 & a trailer. Within our 2 bedroom villa everything provided was of an excellent quality.  Highly recommend for accommodation all year round.

This is a fantastic place,they have accommodation according to your needs,I booked budget ensuite cabin which has two bedrooms,wash+ toilet and a small kitchen,rooms are decent and got everything for a your stay.Parking is available on site
Overall ver happy with the stay.

Eddy and Jus were so accommodating, and made sure we had all the supplies we needed for our stay… even when our emergency call came in after hours, and our need was great! 😥🧻 it wasn’t too much trouble, and it was greatly appreciated! They are a family unit, that looks after family units! Highly recommend, and we’ll be seeing you again soon!
Absolutely amazing place to stay each year for the Bright Rod Run!

We stayed in a budget cabin as a family of four. The space was quite compact — it would’ve been nice to have a small couch, but there probably isn’t enough room for one. The cabin has a bar-sized fridge and a two-burner induction cooktop, so cooking full family meals can be a bit tricky.

The kids’ bunk beds are steel-framed, so watch out for little heads bumping, and there’s nothing stopping young ones (like our 3-year-old) from climbing up the ladder. The playground area is surrounded by sand, which can get messy, especially if the kids go barefoot on the inflatable trampoline.

The pools weren’t heated, so you’ll need a warm, sunny day to enjoy them. There’s not a lot to keep kids entertained on-site, but it’s fine if you’re mainly after a place to sleep for the night. Pretty quiet at night.

We drove from Melbourne on a Fridsay arrived at 1:30, check in at 2. Was told by staff we cannot check in which is fine and then we're told we have to leave the property and park out on the street untill 2pm.

We booked a one bedroom specifically for the seperate bedroom from the kitchen for the little one, this is absolutely not what we got. The images online are not what we got, when we asked they said "nah thats it " and brushed us on.

Our room has 2 towels and were told we charge for more,  with no space to hang a towel after use all hooks are broken.

No ironing board but an iron. No hang space for clothes, 5 beds with 2 total pillows available and only bedding for 3......

Terrible rooms, terrible service, parks decent and if the we weren't lied to or had nicer service could have been a better experience.

Would not recommend,  stay somewhere else
Lovely, well-equipped new room, quiet but convenient location, comfortable bed. We especially liked the spaciousness of the cabin.
Great place
lovely relaxing time

Couldn't have been happier with the service here. Justine was lovely and very accommodating. Other staff member checking us in was also fantastic! Loved the room for a 1 night stay to refresh after camping around the high country.
Close enough to town and appreciated the laundry facilities.

Well priced too.
Very good Will recommend

This was the most disappointing park of a recent family trip. Dated facilities that aren't well maintained. Very basic facilities for kids especially in winter. Great camp kitchen but it wasn't cleaned and it was taken over every night by a large group despite signs saying not to hog the area (This was never policed) No security, there were people not staying at the park wandering around (I know this because one group of people asked us what we thought of the place as they were thinking of staying there) so lock up your valuables if you do stay here. Overall a very average experience. However Bright is beautiful and it's close to the snow fields.
Our cabin was next to the entrance,  therefore you could hear vehicles passing by at night. You get what you paid for. Central heating in the living room.

Stayed for two nights in a deluxe 2-bedroom cabin for a quick ski trip. Service was great! We said we would be arriving after 5pm, so they left our key in the afternoon hours key pick up, and had pre-heated the cabin, and given us details of their onsite drying room for our ski gear.

The cabin was cosy but had nice decor, was toasty warm with the electric fire and split systems, and had everything you’d need.

As we were up at the slopes all day we didn’t get the full park experience, but we would love to stay again to experience the area and the park itself.

The good, drying room for snow gear. $2 driers and a big indoor kitchen area.
The bathrooms 1 star. The ladies doors don't even close properly and when it's 0c there is nothing like open doors in the bathrooms. It's just been left to rack and ruin, the facilities are old and not cleaned well. Cubicles dont close properly. Which is also unnerving with kids. Jumping pillow was closed and we were not notified until arrival (it was clear it wasn't in the last few days..). The sites were average however being a quiet time all campers were jammed  on top of each  despite being room through the camp. Cleaning was minimal we were there 4 nights and the kitchen was cleaned once (you could tell by the build up of dishes and tea towel not changed and dish soap being empty). A large group staying in cabins took over the entire indoor kitchen (2 ovens, dish areas and seating most nights and were there for a few hours each evening). Bright it a lovely town during the day and the river is beautiful and relatively close to snow areas in the winter.
The stay was so cozy to wake up with and woolsworth is nearby. Lovely river stream and parks to walk
Beautiful camp!! Lovely, helpful staff, loved it here!
Easy location to town, couch and bed abit uncomfortable. Great facilities and ease of booking.

unfortunately a very disappointing and uncomfortable experience. Given the number of issues we encountered, I am requesting a refund for our stay.|Upon arrival, we were shocked to find the accommodation in a condition that was far below an acceptable standard. Some of the most concerning issues included:|    •    A used wet towel left hanging in the bathroom, suggesting it had not been properly cleaned between guests.|    •    Black hair in the bathroom, further indicating poor hygiene and cleanliness.|    •    Significant mould and grime buildup on the window seals and in the shower area.|    •    The heater did not work properly, making the stay extremely uncomfortable during the colder hours.|    •    The light in the bathroom did not work, which is not only inconvenient but also a safety concern.|    •    Overall, the entire space had not been cleaned properly, making us feel very uneasy about staying there.||We chose your park based on expectations of a clean and comfortable stay which the website shows photos that are very misleading and are not of the bright caravan park, and unfortunately, this experience fell far short of what is advertised.

Room was not cleaned properly, invaded by ants, dog fur and dust!
Called to get room cleaned but Tasman Carl, wasn’t happy to help as it was at night.

Stayed at bright as a base for Hotham and Falls. The cabin was like something from Harry Potter, looked small from outside and massive inside. Good cooking facilities, heating and plenty of space. Staff were attentive and make check in smooth. 100% would recommend

Just spent 5 nights in the Luxury Holiday Villas. Great space, amazing views and nice and cosy. Definitely takes the chill off when getting back to the snow. Pictures on the website is exactly what you get. Great stay!
Carl and the staff were fantastic, so helpful. Thank you for a great experience.
Nice place to stay. Rooms are great. We had a good experience.
Great cabins

We drove up from Melbourne on a Friday night and stayed one night in the budget cabin to snowboard the next day — and honestly, this place is perfect! The location is spot on, and the cabin had everything we needed: electric blankets (lifesaver), a great heater, hot shower, smart TV, and even a little dining table.

It also has a bunk bed, so it’s ideal if you’re travelling with kids. The interior feels super fresh and way nicer than other budget cabins we’ve stayed in around Bright. It’s such great value for what you get.

We’re already booked to come back in a month with a big group and staying for two nights in the villa — and we seriously can’t wait!

We had a great experience staying at Tasman Holiday Park (South Bright).

The check-in process was smooth and quick—we got our keys without any hassle or long waiting. The reception staff were super friendly and welcoming, which made a great first impression.

Our cabin was spotless—everything was very clean and tidy. The bed sheets, pillows, and towels were fresh, white, and crisp. The kitchen and bathroom were also sparkling clean—honestly, you name it, and it was clean!

A lovely touch: before we arrived, the staff had already turned on the heater in our cabin, which made it warm and cozy as soon as we walked in—such a thoughtful gesture, especially in chilly weather.

We really enjoyed our stay and will definitely come back again. Highly recommend this place if you’re visiting Bright! 🏞️🔥

Stayed four nights in May in a 2 bedroom villa. The villa was very new. Parking spot right next to your villa. Great layout, very clean and aircon, which was great as the weather was cold.
Staff in the office and grounds were very welcoming and friendly.
The park had a games room, an awesome pool and a huge jumping pillow. Very cool.
The park even has a coffee and food van, which did great coffee and toasties.
The park is walking distance to town and multiple walking tracks.
Loved our stay. There is so much we didn’t get to see and do. We will be back at the end of the year for cherry season.
Bright is awesome.

Amazing. John who runs the Tasman Park in Tassie was running the place during our visit was a gentleman with great customer service. We had a great stay. Rooms were great. Will visit the one in Tassie soon.
Great spot. Close to town. Arrived late and heating was already on in the cabin.
Okay for the price, lil small cabins though.

I'm very upset with this accommodation and I haven't even stayed here. My grievance is targeted at their disregard of the contract that they have with the 3rd party provider that they advertise with and with whom I had booked this accommodation. The understanding was that I wouldn't need to pay until we arrived to stay there.  Free cancellation was also part of the package up to near the date of staying. I cancelled the accommodation early up to a month before as i found accommodation more suited to us. Unfortunately, though, Tasmsn Holiday parks decided that they would take the full amount earlier than was advertised and contracted. Now I have had to wait 2 pay cycles to receive the refund, and on top, I'm being charged interest on it from my card.  I should not have been charged that early when I had chosen to pay when I arrived at the accommodation. It may be a great place to stay, but after this encounter, I don't know if I would ever want to anyway.

Not a great experience
The staff were pretty rude. They ask for a fee for a late checkout, and when I mentioned that it felt a bit unfair, the woman responded in a really unpleasant way. Honestly, they don’t seem to understand how to treat tourists or make people feel welcome.
Recently stayed here over the Easter break (2025). The staff were very friendly and accommodating. The facilities were clean along with the park as a whole. We'll definitely be back.

Great park for our Easter trip away with our caravan. Pricey on peak times but worth it considering we booked really close to Easter,
Small sites but just enough space.
Plenty of kids enjoying themselves over the Easter break,
Bright is absolutely stunning this time of year and full of colour so be prepared for massive crowds and plenty of tourist and I mean it’s packed…
We will definitely be back again.
The park itself is very nice with a good size pool, Jumping pillow, Play Ground, games room, lounge area etc
Clean amenities
Fire pits allowed
Well taken care of.

Overall the camp lacks grass/concrete and is full of dust. The site provided to us had specifically no grass at all and had no tree shade. Also, it was right next to a common sitting area. Due to this, our tent attracted lot of dust, made it impossible for us to stay inside the tent from late morning until sunset and made it difficult for us to sleep until late night as there used to be folks sitting in the common area and talking loudly until 10 pm. This site should not be allocated to anyone and should be kept vacant due to the reasons mentioned. Was a bad overall experience.

I stayed up here for 5 nights with my caravan. I had a private ensuite powered site. It was so handy. I really enjoyed my time up here.

Having visited the area quite a few times over the past 20 years, I hadn't actually used this park before. I will again if I get the chance. Great location, not far from the town centre, and great facilities

Thanks Tasman

We stayed in a cabin that for starters looked completely different to the pictures you see of south bright tasman Park, it was rough, Bed was junk and was bolted to floor however the bolts were loose so it was like a rocking horse , toilet leaking water, floor was filthy from the cleaners walking around on it with dirty feet from the gravel outside, The worst part was that before we arrived reception made us call them for a bond because apparently booking.com hide the credit card details I paid with...so I called them and paid the bond I would never get back because apparently we left the room messy!! Turns out the picture reception gave us was time stamped from a day earlier!!! So not us!! Still waiting on this fraudulent transaction from Tasman parks to come back to me over two weeks later now!!!
P.s mountain bikers, your not allowed to bring your bikes into cabins, we didn't have our bikes this trip but your expected to leave them in a pretty accessible area to everyone , i would never go back to this dump, and I'd recommend you shouldn't either!!

We enjoyed 2 nights in a pet friendly budget cabin. The housekeeping staff did a fantastic job the cabin was one of the cleanest cabins we have ever stayed in. The facilities in the park looked awesome and staff were friendly
Close to township. Lovely walkway near the river.

We discovered Tasman South Bright after having to leave a prior booking at a park a few kms out of town (where we couldn't pitch our tent because the pegs wouldn’t go into the ground and the management didn’t care). Despite the stress of losing our booking during the busy Buffalo Stampede weekend, it turned out to be a blessing! Tasman South Bright is fantastic—great facilities, lovely sites, and friendly, helpful staff (Lisa and Steve in particular!). It's a small but well-equipped park with clean amenities and an excellent camp kitchen/recreation room. The park is quiet yet just a short stroll to town. We will definitely return. Highly recommend for anyone visiting Bright!

We just spent a weekend in a cabin in the newly developed precinct off Delaney Avenue, and it was amazing.  The cabins are so clean, tidy, modern and with all the home comforts. Great quality appliances, with a coffee machine too, what more do you need.

We were opposite a pool and spa, which was separate to the main pool in the larger area, which is kept separate via a gate, so a bonus having 2 pools to choose from.  Along with bikes to hire and a jumping pillow, the kids were very entertained.  Highly recommend, and worth paying a little extra for a cabin in this new section.

We discovered Tasman South Bright after having to leave a prior booking at a park a few kms out of town (where we couldn't pitch our tent because the pegs wouldn’t go into the ground and the management didn’t care). Despite the stress of losing our booking during the busy Buffalo Stampede weekend, it turned out to be a blessing! Tasman South Bright is fantastic—great facilities, lovely sites, and friendly, helpful staff (Lisa and Steve in particular!). It's a small but well-equipped park with clean amenities and an excellent camp kitchen/recreation room. The park is quiet yet just a short stroll to town. We will definitely return. Highly recommend for anyone visiting Bright!

Easily one of the best caravan parks we’ve stayed at. Awesome amount of shade over the powered sites and the camp kitchen was incredibly well-maintained. Only downside was that there wasn’t a lot of grass on the sites but other than that it was perfect.

Stayed here with my partner for a week an absolutely loved it! The park has a pool with waterslide and water jets (it’s very cold though), jumping pillow and playground, public camp kitchen for campers and is very clean we stayed in the budget cabin which was perfect for us and had kitchen and bathroom as well as 3 bunk beds for children.

The park is lovely, with a pool very family friendly.
The villas are well priced and clean and new.
The customer service is excellent

Stayed 3 nights in a 3 bedroom cabin. The rooms were so small you could hardly move. The beds were lumpy and uncomfortable. The bedrooms had minimal wardrobe space with not even any hooks to hang things and that included the bathroom. The unit itself was dirty. It had coffee splash marks on the walls that would have been obvious to the cleaner to wipe off. The floor had definitely not been washed which was agreed upon by all of us. 3 bedrooms for 6 adults and only 1 spare toilet roll!!! The sofa was rediculous, once you sat in it you sunk so much you had to be helped out. The facilities of the property were very good though and the maintenance man was exceptional and very helpful.
Great park & amenities. We stayed in Spring and enjoyed the local markets, wine, food and natural beauty. The coffee can was a life saver!
One if the nicest caravan parks i have been to. Great pool, jumping pillow. Staff were awesome. Will be there again soon.

The holiday park is very very good and amenity good.. common toilets clean but not one clean it the next day.. as it waa raining and all the floor has mud etc.. on the other hand specifically one lady is very very rude and racial and ready to fight at loudest.. she had the problem with everything. All the reviews related to staff are correct and the staff is not at all supportive... they ruined the value of the park by behaviour but holiday park is good..

I cannot believe the staff they have hired, particularly this senior woman, she had been overly rude and unkind towards all of us, as we were 6 families with kids. She made the kids wait to use the jumping pillow till 9 am, on the pretext of not having the keys, though the signage said opening hours were from 8 am. She also did close all the kitchen doors except just leaving one panel open, though the cleaning was finished around an hour ago. She came across as an aggressive person and was throwing the cleaning tools to show her anger and frustration.She should be given certain trainings as how to work and behave hospitality industry.
Overall, the park is well kept and really nice, with big roomy kitchen indoors and outdoors. The pool is equipped with couple of small slides a s relatively of larger size.
However, we would not come back due to her behaviour as it did cause anguish among us.

Everything you want in a pretty leafy setting. Powered sites and cabins. Cabins are clean and comfortable. Laundry was great although $6 for a wash is expensive (only $2 for a dry… go figure!). Parking outside cabin is really convenient if loading/unloading from a car. Communal facilities are well appointed. Pools look great. It’s a 15min walk to town. Highly recommended.
We hired a campsite, and while the kitchen and bathrooms were very clean, we unfortunately had a negative experience with the old lady there, who was quite rude.

Friendly staff overall but very expensive especially when you consider their van sites are very small.  Most we have ever paid for a site anywhere. Even with our van parked right back to the line between our site and the site behind us we were forced to have the tow bar on the van right out to the edge of the driveway.   VERY tight access to hitch and unhitch van.  Had to push up against the bushes of the cabin opposite us to hitch up. I had put in our van length plus an extra 0.5m  when booking so the van size was clear. Spoke to the lady at reception who could see our van and site as it was in front of the reception and said the site was too tight but she said she was happy with how it looked.  PLUS NO parking on your van site for your car.  It was busy but there was no toilet paper in the men's toilets on the Saturday.

I booked a 1 night stay. Receptionist was lovely and very helpful.
The 2 br Cabin was quite old, but clean. Not worth the $299 for a night.
My problem was that the park is cramped with large families and a HEAP of loud kids. Some families even had their dogs there.
Kids running around all day and evening screaming, talking loud. Very cramped park. I open the drapes and the other peoples camp is right outside the window. Every window you look out someone can look in  to the cabin
No really for people who want a bit more privacy and a more quieter place to relax.
Every man and his dog was there.. kids running in front of moving cars. Pool was busy with kids.
Dads walking around with their beers.. mums were all gathered chatting about their hubbies.. ect..
Location was good. Not far from main street.
3/10 for me.
I Stayed during end of school holidays in Jan.
Pretty good for a short stay. Plenty of amenities. There's a good pool for kids with a water slide. Pretty central location.
Located just outside of the town centre. Our cabin was spacious, clean and comfortable. Kids loved the pool

I spent past few days at this park in a cabin..
On arrival the manager ( Gina) The Smiling Assassin made a point of telling me they have and have had very feral people staying in the park. I was told there is a curfew and all patrons must be back inside cabins by 10pm...
I also witnessed her telling children there are some very naughty children in the park who are not supervised by their parents..
I got to the cabin to find the keys didn't open the door so I was told not to lock the security door because I wouldn't be able to open it again 😲
Next the air-conditioner was barely blowing any cool air, sitting at 25 degrees.
Gina was not impressed that I complained telling me this is an acceptable temperature.
The bed mattress was hard as a rock.
One of the morning's I asked for some fresh linen, I was told by Gina 'this is not a motel' linen does not get changed, all linen is to last the whole stay. ( even though paying same price per night as a motel)
One night the managers put on a sausage sizzle at a cost..
Gina had on black gloves for cooking,  however she kept those gloves on walking through the park after cooking to the reception,  then back to kitchen area, eating with the gloves on, scratching her head and then handling raw sausages again to cook..
The water slide was turned off at around 7pm and pool closed by 8pm even though it was still very hot 🌞
Reception is closed at 5pm..
Overall very disappointing,  this woman should clearly NOT be running a holiday park...
A very rude woman.
I told her before I left she needs some training in customer service 🙄
However the new girl who had just started was lovely 😊

Small cramped room. Air con was cheap did not cool down the whole place. Room was so hot, could not cool down, we couldn’t sleep. bed was as hard as a rock. Little sofa also hard as rock. The bench outside we could not use as it was falling apart. Loud children screaming all hours of the night. Crowded. Really over priced for what it was. No iron. Please throw out plastic cutting boards as well very unhygienic. I was pretty dissatisfied.

I recently stayed at Tasman Holiday Park in Bright, and my experience was deeply disappointing due to serious staff and management issues, particularly with a night manager named Jenna (if I spelled it correctly). Her behaviour was rude, aggressive, and intimidating, especially towards women and children in our group. She yelled at children during dinner, harassed women in the kitchen, and created such a hostile atmosphere that my 9-year-old son couldn’t sleep during the night.
Jenna’s overreactions escalated to alarming levels. In one instance, she threatened to call the police over a minor issue, and also involved her husband to intimidate a friend further with physical violence. She rushed at us aggressively, refused to stop confronting one family member, and made it her mission to lecture each individual in our group. Her approach was extreme and controlling, treating everyone as if they were subordinates under her authority. It was an outrageous display of unprofessionalism.
At times, her behaviour even hinted at potential racial discrimination, which must be investigated immediately by management.
Beyond the staff issues, the accommodation itself was inadequate. My unit lacked extra rubbish bags, provided only one toilet roll, and the TV was dangerously placed, posing a serious risk to small children. Further, the water (the units in the new section/area of the park) had an unusual taste, resembling mud or soil. This raised concerns about its safety and cleanliness for consumption.
Tasman Holiday Park urgently needs to improve its staff training and ensure compliance with Australian laws, including the Caravan Parks and Movable Dwellings Regulations 2020 (Vic) and Australian Consumer Law (ACL). Yelling at children and families and fostering a traumatic environment is entirely unacceptable.
Sadly, I cannot recommend this park to anyone seeking a safe and relaxing holiday. My experience was deeply disappointing, and I strongly urge others to be cautious when considering this destination.

I had an extremely disappointing and distressing experience with your night manager, Jenna. Her behavior was not only unprofessional but unnecessarily aggressive, leaving a profoundly negative impression on me and my group. Rather than handling situations with professionalism and respect, she treated us in an authoritarian and hostile manner, completely disregarding basic customer service standards.

Despite the fact that it was not even 10 p.m., she rudely intervened and ruined our party. She refused to cooperate in any way, escalating the situation instead of resolving it. Shockingly, she went so far as to bring her husband into the matter, who threatened us with physical violence. To make things even worse, Jenna threatened to call the police, which terrified our families, especially the children. They were so shaken by the ordeal that they couldn’t sleep well that night.

On another occasion, I witnessed Jenna ordering a client to move their car from a parking spot that had no signage indicating it was reserved for staff. This behavior reflects a complete lack of fairness and consideration for customers.

Overall, Jenna's actions were not only unprofessional but deeply unsettling. I strongly urge you to address this issue immediately to ensure such incidents do not occur again and to create a safe, welcoming environment for all your guests.
We loved this place.  So lovely and clean and they even had a pool and a hot tub surrounded by mountains. We're going back! 🙂
Booked a cabin for overnight stay. It was very affordable and cosy. The reception was really friendly,very clean and nice location too.

Super happy with the location, amenities and cabin was very well made, nice design, super clean neat and comfortable.

Everything needed was there.

Felt like home!

Loved it!

The cabin provided was not fit for humans. We couldnot stay in it as the cabin bed was dodgy and unstable. It was highlighted to the staff. Further, The bunk bed was full of ants and there were dead bugs all over the cabin.

Property Manager accompanied us and I showed her all the bugs, mosquitos and ants. She came with a mortien spray to kill them all.The cabin was never occupied and keys were returned as the place was unfit for humans.

The double bed was unstable, the bunk beds were full of ants and no on can sleep in those cramped spaces.

Property Manager suggested to reach out to booking.com for refund as this was booked through them. She also mentioned if it was directly booked she could have processed it as we were not staying. I know its all in properties hand but they are playing smart and not returning our hard earned money and left us without accomodation on our holiday.

It is like charging fee for no service.

They claim themselves as a 4 star property but didnot had the blanket for the mattress in the cabin (Basics were missing), I wish google review had an option of a ZERO star

Never ever book this property if you really want to enjoy beautiful Bright.
The facilities are awesome and the staff are really friendly. I would recommend this holiday park accommodation to anyone.
Love our stay. Cozy place ! Staff were very helpful.

Comfy cabins. Good location.
Play areas for the kids.
Stayed here with family during our Mt Hotham trip. Would love to visit again.

Very cosy and modern camp kitchen/ dining area with amazing looking pool (too cold for us to utilise during our stay). The bbq was pretty average (turned off quickly and then didn’t start back up properly again) but very happy with the kitchen having kettles, toasters and lots of heaters and space.
Communal toilets/showers are very squishy and feel a bit uninviting. Quite clean though. Also there is a family room with a full size bath for the kids.

Pretty close to snow peaks and fairly quiet except for some cars going past. Bright is quite a nice town to stay in.

We didn’t stay in any of the cabins but they have quite a few that look super fancy and modern. We did however have ensuite sites and they were pretty average. Needing a bit of an update with slightly more room in the shower to accommodate larger individuals. Toilet also seemed to block easily, needing to be flushed both before and after use. Did love the heat lights for extra warmth.

The sites got very washed out and muddy when it rained and the ground went very slushy.

The photo advertisements for this park are very misleading as some photos aren’t even of the park, they are of the river in the main part of town (picture of river).

No problems for the particular house that we got. Nice that there's a decorative led fireplace and there were a number of heating options which really helps when you're checking in during winter. Nice location to stay in going to Mt. Hotham. Overall a good experience.
Cabins are good size, i found it little out dated but functional. Playzone had enough options to keep you occupied for couple of hours. Close to the river.

Have stayed in a cabin here once before, we thought we would upgrade to a villa last visit that sleeps 6.
The villa was spacious and comfortable. There were no instructions for TVs and electric fire. The cutlery and crockery supplied was a bit ad hoc. If we did have 6 staying there wasn't enough cutlery or crockery for every person. The cooking utensils were also a bit light on for making a good meal in the villa. Then the chux for washing dishes had been cut in half and disintegrated in one day, had to ask for another. Weren't that impressed for the price. Could be better.

Very unfriendly to bunk bed tenants.
We booked a 3 beds room and it was okay for the two double rooms, but there was no sockets in bunk beds room, and we had to make our own bed, even the sheets were not fitted the mattress!
The bunk beds were the dirtiest bed I had ever slept on. Four corners were dusted with yellow stains, the bed and the quilt looked like never cleaned, and the cloth hanger provided was broken!
I would rather give zero star if there was such an option.

Paid a lot of money for tiny cabin, but location was perfect. My only issue was with the older lady on reception...the grumpiness was unbelievable.  Very hostile attitude towards every request and query.
Very clean, great service, awesome location.

We stayed at this park and was very disappointed. A very dusty, dusty and dirty park. Sites very unkempt. For the money, not worth it, in fact left a day early.
Over rated.
Highly recommended, love location very convenient and affordable. Will book again .

I cannot believe how caravan parks can promote their accommodation and not deliver what we as customers expect. For the cost of accommodation and what we experienced it was a total disappointment, the lounge deserved to be at the tip, even with two cushions stacked on top of one another it was not worth sitting on, the curtains in one of the bedrooms were a disgrace, the basin in the bathroom was blocked and the pressure for hot water to come through was non existent. The new managers who had only been there for five days made the decision to put us into one of the newer cabins which we appreciated. But please, please, please, stop promoting accommodation as being great when it definitely does not pass muster.

Booked a powered ensuite site through G’day Parks.  Their advertisement showed a lovely grassy site, perfect for pitching a tent on. The reality was a stoney, dusty, barren site with a faulty external power point. Very disappointing and false advertising.
Perfect accommodation. Felt like home away from home especially their villas.
Lovely clean facilities. They have everything you need. Quiet and peaceful, I slept well. Iv been here a few times now & will return. Each time I've stayed it's been nice

Bright-style 🌲 🛖🏔 - Location was ideal, and environment was quiet (mid week) for a stay in one of their superior cabins. Well appointed, and clean. Park is going through some solid upgrades, and look forward to seeing it all come together.
We had a lovely stay at the Tasman Holiday Park. The Cabin we booked was clean and new. Great facilities and lovely staff. The coffee van is a great addition. We will definitely be back!

Absolutely horrible |Had the sites that backed on to the permanent residence. Night 1 got I trouble for 3 people talking past 10pm. Day 2 nearly had 2 kids taken out by a van as they were walking to the toilets. Caretaker of the park was sitting at the entrance of the men’s toilets after dark highly inappropriate for an adult to occupy that area after hours. Day 3 abused for driving too fast into the park (yes over the 8klm hr speed limit but not the accused speed to make the occupants of the vehicle to reach the roof of the car!) same night complained about music that has been loud and played since past 10pm (currently 1154pm) caretaker of the park wanted to start an argument with us for complaining about said noise after 10pm. ||Do not recommend staying here! Over priced, not welcoming, facilities are well under average and the service and respect from staff is absolutely ridiculous.

Noisiest caravan park that I have ever been to. Every night there were big groups partying with multiple blue tooth speakers blaring. They have a 10pm curfew for noise but several nights it didn't quieten down until almost midnight. Great for families with older teens and big groups wanting to party but I wouldn't recommend it for families wanting a nice relaxing holiday. There were no staff or security around after hours unless you specifically call with a complaint or concern.

We had a great time, and we really appreciated the efforts the staff went to ensuring that their guests had a fun and hassle free experience.
They go the extra mile for the kids, and the facilities are in excellent condition.

Beautiful park, nice big trees, friendly staff, well sized & elevated caravan lots.
Needs better speed humps to deter speeding drivers utilising the cabins. Barking dog of nearby neighbour was a bit annoying.
beautiful Bright...so much to do

I had a booking at Tasman Holiday Park for what I thought was June this year. I arrive at reception to discover that I am a month early. I start panicking as it’s a very popular time of year for tourists and worry that I am letting my friends down who have joined me on the 5 hour trip. However, I am met with an amazing receptionist who tried everything possible to find me new accomodation as they were booked out. I was then refunded for the error I had made and continued on to our new accomodation.  I was so so grateful for the kindness this lady offered in my moment on panic and we went on to have a wonderful time in Bright! I will definitely be booking at Tasman Holiday Park next time I visit.

Great for kids and parents as there is a pool and bouncing castle. Short walk into town. Dog friendly. You can have a fire. Small sites so hardly any room to park your car. Unfortunately there were too many noisy kids running around the park that spoiled our stay.

Absolutely lovely place to stay for a holiday. The cabin was great and on-site facilities terrific.  Love the coffee caravan! Staff are very friendly and helpful. Walking distance to town. I will definitely be back. Highly recommended A+
Great service
I cant find zero star to review very rude and threatening to call Police Lady at reception very unprofessional

The owner forgot to provide linen upon our arrival. However, they gave us some at 11pm when we arrived. There is a large table at the kitchen for big groups. Pool table and table tennis are available. There is also a pool and other entertainments for kids and teenagers. Rooms are a little old but clean. We enjoyed our stay there for a snowtrip weekend!
Our cabin was well fitted out and the facility was in a quiet location.
Nice stay on our visit to mt hotham and falls creek, convinient to stay in bright and visit around.. the checkin was easy ... the fire pit offered was great as well
It was good and clean. . .
Great service and stay. Honest housekeeping staff that found my wallet after my stay. Thanks Amelia 🙏
Excellent place
Great park, excellent camp kitchen/recreation area. Easy walk to town.

Had wonderful experience.
Friendly staff. Our rooms were nice and tidy.
We had memorable stay...
Thank you
Great park. Friendly staff!
